pattern: Because no two added roads ever partially overlap, every one either nests inside an earlier road or sits fully outside it, so a union-find 'next still-needed city' pointer can retire each city at most once across all queries.
hints: The no-crossing guarantee means every added road's span either sits fully inside a previous one, is fully disjoint from it, or fully contains it — the spans form a strict hierarchy, never a tangle.

Picture the shortest path as a shrinking chain of 'still necessary' city indices; adding a road from u to v removes every still-necessary index strictly between them from that chain, whether or not one of them was already an endpoint of an earlier road.

Maintain a union-find style 'find the next still-necessary index at or after x' pointer so each city is retired at most once in total across every query; after a query the path length is just the chain's remaining size minus one.

edge_inputs.description: minimum city count with the only legal query
a single query covering the whole path
a nested query inside an already-shortcut range is a no-op
a later, larger query re-shortcuts a smaller earlier one
a chain of disjoint shortcuts laid end to end

stress.description: random laminar family of shortcuts (nested or disjoint, never crossing)
